---
title: "Research"
toc: true
toc-location: left
preview: images/photo.jpg
lightbox: true
execute: 
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

My research focuses on how food retail markets and consumer food decisions interact and contribute to economic outcomes across communities. I also study the role of data infrastructure in supporting evidence-based food policy, including efforts to increase transparency and public access to high-quality datasets through initiatives like [DemocratizingData.ai](https://democratizingdata.ai/).

Across these areas, I use high-resolution data, including scanner data, retail census data, and other micro-level datasets, to study how market structure, firm behavior, public policy, and information systems affect consumers, retailers, and researchers.

[Download CV](cv.html){.btn .btn-primary}

---

# Food Retail Markets, Competition, and Food Access

This area of my research examines food retailer competition, pricing, and variation in the food options available across communities. Much of this work focuses on the changing food retail landscape, including dollar stores, food deserts, retail markups, store profitability, and public policies that influence food access.

## Selected publications

**Dollar-Store Impact on Local Labor Markets and Retail Activity**  
Chenarides, L., T. J. Richards, Z. Rutledge, and J. Pender. 2025. *Food Policy*.  
[Published version](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.foodpol.2025.102827)

**Retail Markups and Discount-Store Entry**  
Chenarides, L., M. I. Gomez, T. J. Richards, and K. Yonezawa. 2023. *Review of Industrial Organization*.  
[Published version](https://doi.org/10.1007/s11151-023-09926-w)

**Dynamic Model of Entry: Dollar Stores**  
Chenarides, L., M. Çakir, and T. J. Richards. 2023. *American Journal of Agricultural Economics*.  
[Published version](https://doi.org/10.1111/ajae.12404)

**Food Retail Profits, Competition, and the Great Recession**  
Cleary, R. O., and L. Chenarides. 2022. *Agribusiness: An International Journal*.  
[Published version](https://doi.org/10.1002/agr.21743)

**Dollar Stores and Food Deserts**  
Chenarides, L., C. Cho, R. M. Nayga, Jr., and M. R. Thomsen. 2021. *Applied Geography*.  
[Published version](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.apgeog.2021.102497)

**If You Build Them…Will it Matter? Food Stores’ Presence and Perceived Barriers to Purchasing Healthy Foods in the Northeastern U.S.**  
Chenarides, L., A. Bonanno, and A. Palmer. 2020. *Applied Economic Perspectives and Policy*.  
[Published version](https://doi.org/10.1002/aepp.13098)

**Documenting the Link between Poor Food Access and Less Healthy Product Assortment across the U.S.**  
Chenarides, L., and E. C. Jaenicke. 2018. *Applied Economic Perspectives and Policy*.  
[Published version](https://doi.org/10.1093/aepp/ppy018)

**Store Formats, Market Structure, and Consumers’ Food Shopping Decisions**  
Volpe, R. J., III, E. C. Jaenicke, and L. Chenarides. 2018. *Applied Economic Perspectives and Policy*.  
[Published version](https://doi.org/10.1093/aepp/ppx033)

**Store Profitability and Public Policies to Improve Food Access in Non-Metro U.S. Counties**  
Cleary, R. O., A. Bonanno, L. Chenarides, and S. J. Goetz. 2018. *Food Policy*.  
[Published version](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.foodpol.2017.12.004)

## Working papers and ongoing research

**Market Structure and Price Markups in Food Retail**  
Chenarides, L., J. Lowrey, R. J. Volpe, and A. Okrent.

**Food Retailer Pricing Behavior Under Anti-Price Gouging Laws: Evidence from Scanner Data**  
Chenarides, L., and T. J. Richards.

**Dollar Stores, SNAP, and Food Access**  
Chenarides, L., and C. Ambrozek.

---

# Consumer Food Choices and Market Trends

This area of my research studies how consumers respond to changing food products, market disruptions, supply chain shocks, public health events, and food trends. Topics include plant-based foods, COVID-19 food behavior, fruit and vegetable markets, urban agriculture, and food safety compliance.

## Selected publications

**Relationship between Knowledge and Compliance with Safety Measures: Evidence from COVID-19**  
Cetik, N., C. Grebitus, and L. Chenarides. 2025. *Applied Economic Perspectives and Policy*.  
[Published version](https://doi.org/10.1002/aepp.70031)

**Grocery Stockouts and Seller Performance in Amazon’s Marketplaces**  
Etumnu, C., L. Chenarides, and E. C. Jaenicke. 2025. *Agribusiness*.  
[Published version](https://doi.org/10.1002/agr.22051)

**Consumer Spending on Plant-Based Meat Alternatives**  
Chenarides, L., J. Cuffey, W. Li, S. Zhao, and B. Adamo. 2024. *Choices*.  
[Published version](https://shorturl.at/WAANF)

**Consumer Spending Patterns for Plant-Based Meat Alternatives**  
Cuffey, J., L. Chenarides, W. Li, and S. Zhao. 2022. *Applied Economic Perspectives and Policy*.  
[Published version](https://doi.org/10.1002/aepp.13280)

**A Calibrated Choice Experiment Method**  
Chenarides, L., C. Grebitus, J. L. Lusk, and I. Printezis. 2022. *European Review of Agricultural Economics*.  
[Published version](https://doi.org/10.1093/erae/jbac011)

**COVID-19 Impact on Fruit and Vegetable Markets: One Year Later**  
Chenarides, L., T. J. Richards, and B. Rickard. 2021. *Canadian Journal of Agricultural Economics*.  
[Published version](http://doi.org/10.1111/cjag.12272)

**Food Consumption Behavior During COVID-19**  
Chenarides, L., C. Grebitus, J. Lusk, and I. Printezis. 2021. *Agribusiness: An International Journal*.  
[Published version](http://dx.doi.org/10.1002/agr.21679)

**Who Practices Urban Agriculture? An Empirical Analysis of Participation Before and During the COVID-19 Pandemic**  
Chenarides, L., C. Grebitus, J. Lusk, and I. Printezis. 2021. *Agribusiness: An International Journal*.  
[Published version](http://dx.doi.org/10.1002/agr.21675)

**COVID-19 and Food Supply Chains**  
Chenarides, L., M. Manfredo, and T. J. Richards. 2021. *Applied Economic Perspectives and Policy*.  
[Published version](https://doi.org/10.1002/aepp.13085)

**Consumers’ Perception of Urban Agriculture: An Exploratory Study**  
Grebitus, C., L. Chenarides, R. Muenich, and A. Mahalov. 2020. *Frontiers in Sustainable Food Systems*.  
[Published version](https://doi.org/10.3389/fsufs.2020.00079)

## Working papers and ongoing research

**Supply Chain Resilience and Food Supply Chains**  
Manfredo, M., S. Webster, T. J. Richards, and L. Chenarides.

**Inventory Competition and the Cost of a Stockout**  
Richards, T. J., S. F. Hamilton, E. Rabinovich, and L. Chenarides.

**Using Modern Portfolio Theory to Optimize Farmers Market Activity**  
David, R., L. Chenarides, and M. Mallory.

---

# Data Democratization and Evidence-Based Policy

This area of my research studies how access to data, research infrastructure, and publication systems affect the questions researchers can ask and the evidence available for public policy. This work includes data linkages, evidence-based policymaking, machine learning, federal data infrastructure, and open-access publication models.

## Selected publications

**A Review of Data Linkages for Policy-Informing Research in Food and Agricultural Economics**  
Chenarides, L., A. S. Hanks, J. Berard, A. Carlson, G. Davis, and A. Finaret. 2025. *Food Policy*.  
[Published version](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.foodpol.2025.102996)

**Machine Learning in Applied Economics and Agribusiness: Emerging Applications and Integration with Traditional Methods**  
Maruejols, L., X. Yu, and L. Chenarides. 2025. *International Food and Agribusiness Management Review*.  
[Published version](https://doi.org/10.22434/ifamr.0001)

**Federal Economic Data on Local and Regional Food Producers**  
Bauman, A., B. B. R. Jablonski, D. A. Yeh, L. Chenarides, and D. Thilmany McFadden. 2024. *Journal of the Agricultural and Applied Economics Association*.  
[Published version](https://doi.org/10.1002/jaa2.131)

**Creating Engagements: Bringing the User into Data Democratization**  
Chenarides, L. 2024. *Harvard Data Science Review*.  
[Published version](https://doi.org/10.1162/99608f92.24c26aa9)

**An Outlook on Evidence-Based Policymaking**  
Chenarides, L., A. Hanks, A. Finaret, A. Carlson, and G. Davis. 2024. *Choices*.  
[Published version](https://shorturl.at/hgdGc)

**Grand Challenge: To Ensure That All People Have Safe, Affordable, Accessible, and Acceptable Food for Leading a Healthy and Active Life**  
Wilson, N., L. Chenarides, J. Kolodinsky, and K. Boys. 2024. *Choices*.  
[Published version](https://shorturl.at/nK9vt)

## Working papers and ongoing research

**Data-Usage Descriptors as Search Metadata: The Case of Food Security Data and the National Data Platform, 2015–2025**  
Chenarides, L., R. Ladislau, M. Parashar, D. Hook, S. Porter, and J. Lane.

**The Economic Trade-offs between Subscription-Based and Open-Access Publication Models**  
Bryan, C., L. Chenarides, J. I. Lane, and M. Wang.

---

# Teaching and public-facing scholarship

Some of my work also focuses on teaching, experiential learning, and public-facing communication.

**Integrating Experiential Learning into a Systems Framework: An Application to Promote Food Deserts and Food Access Concepts Among College Students**  
Shaw-Hughner, R., C. Dumitrescu, L. Chenarides, C. Wharton, and G. Lacagnina. 2021. *Applied Economics Teaching Resources*.
[Published version](https://ageconsearch.umn.edu/record/316504/files/AETR_2020_039RRRPDF_v3.pdf)

**Using a Sensory Learning Framework to Design Effective Curricula: Evidence from Indigenous Nutrition Education Programs**  
Green, K., and L. Chenarides. 2020. *Sustainability*.  
[Published version](https://doi.org/10.3390/su12177077)

**Are dollar stores the villains they’ve been made out to be?**  
Chenarides, L. 2022. *Fast Company*.  
[Article](https://www.fastcompany.com/90780087/are-dollar-stores-the-villains-theyve-been-made-out-to-be)

**Dollar Store: Friend or Foe?**  
Chenarides, L. 2019. *Arizona Food Industry Journal*.

---

# Full publication list

For a complete and current list of publications, working papers, grants, and presentations, please see my [CV](cv.html).
